# Regression test: annotation follows aisle_angle_deg rotation.
#
# The canvas-anchored AbstractFrame rotates when aisle_angle_deg
# changes. A grid vertex at (xi, yi) in the frame moves in an arc as
# the angle sweeps — but its abstract index is unchanged, so an
# annotation keyed by index keeps pointing at the "same" grid
# intersection in the rotated layout.

polygon outer
0,0
400,0
400,400
0,400
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

set stalls_per_face=4
----
set stalls_per_face=4

debug use_abstract_stamp=true
----
debug use_abstract_stamp=true

# Baseline.
generate
----
total_stalls: 184

# Delete an interior grid vertex.
annotation abstract-delete-vertex region=0x1000000000000 x=-3 y=3
----
annotation abstract-delete-vertex region=0x0001000000000000 x=-3 y=3

generate
----
total_stalls: 192

# Rotate the aisle angle. Every aisle rotates around origin (0,0),
# including the targeted vertex. The annotation still resolves
# because (region, -3, 3) names the same grid cell in the rotated
# frame.
set aisle_angle_deg=75
----
set aisle_angle_deg=75

generate
----
total_stalls: 192

screenshot
----
